description: Add periodic re-evaluation of plugin isReady() so the side panel state stays accurate when a plugin becomes unavailable without a page navigation (e.g., token expires, user logs out via SPA transition)

userStories.title: Add periodic isReady re-evaluation for active plugin tabs
userStories.description: As a user, I want the side panel plugin state to accurately reflect whether a plugin is ready, even when the auth token expires or is cleared without a page navigation, so that the green/red indicator is trustworthy
acceptanceCriteria: Plugin tabs that were previously `ready` are re-probed periodically (e.g., every 30-60 seconds)
If isReady() returns false during a periodic probe, the tab state transitions from `ready` to `unavailable` and a `tab.stateChanged` notification is sent to the MCP server
If isReady() returns true during a periodic probe for a previously `unavailable` tab, it transitions to `ready`
The polling interval is configurable via a constant (not hardcoded in multiple places)
Polling does NOT run for tabs in `closed` state (no matching tab exists)
Polling stops when the extension's WebSocket is disconnected (no point probing if we can't notify the server)
The periodic probe uses the same 5-second timeout as the existing probeTabReadiness function
userStories.notes:
The readiness state is managed in `platform/browser-extension/src/tab-state.ts`. Key functions:

- `probeTabReadiness(tabId, adapterFile)` (line 64) — calls `isReady()` via `chrome.scripting.executeScript` with a 5-second timeout
- `computePluginTabState(plugin)` (line 117) — computes per-tab state for a plugin, calls `probeTabReadiness` for each matching tab
- `sendTabSyncAll()` (line 143) — computes state for ALL plugins after `sync.full`
- `notifyAffectedPlugins(tabId, url)` (line 256) — re-computes state for plugins affected by a tab event, diffs against `lastKnownState`, and sends `tab.stateChanged` only when state actually changed
- `lastKnownState` (line 26) — Map<pluginName, serialized state> used for diffing

Currently `isReady()` is re-evaluated ONLY on:
1. `chrome.tabs.onUpdated` (URL change or page load complete) — via `checkTabChanged`
2. `chrome.tabs.onRemoved` / `onReplaced` — via `checkTabRemoved`
3. `sync.full` from MCP server — via `sendTabSyncAll`
4. `plugin.update` — direct re-computation
5. Tool/resource/prompt dispatch — just-in-time gate check (but does NOT update `lastKnownState`)

The gap: if a plugin's auth token expires or is cleared while the page stays open (no navigation), the side panel keeps showing the stale `ready` state until the next page load or WebSocket reconnect.

Implementation approach:
- Add a `setInterval` in `background.ts` or `tab-state.ts` that periodically calls `notifyAffectedPlugins` or a similar function for all tabs with `ready` or `unavailable` state
- Use a reasonable interval (30-60 seconds) — this is a background probe, not a real-time check
- Only probe tabs that are currently in `ready` or `unavailable` state (skip `closed`)
- Guard against concurrent probes (if a probe is already running from a tab event, skip the periodic one)
- Make sure to clear the interval when the WebSocket disconnects and restart it on reconnect

Alternative approach: Instead of polling ALL plugins, only poll plugins whose tabs haven't had a navigation event in the last N seconds. This reduces unnecessary work for actively-used tabs where navigation events already trigger re-evaluation.

IMPORTANT: The `isReady()` call involves `chrome.scripting.executeScript` which is asynchronous and can fail (tab closed, adapter not injected, etc.). Handle all error cases gracefully — a failed probe should not crash the background service worker or leave stale state.
